curriculum.subject: Chemistry
curriculum.overallDescription: 10 units, 9 chapters, and 0 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Express the concentration of solutions using various units
Describe vapour pressure of liquid solutions
Distinguish between ideal and non-ideal solutions
Apply colligative properties to determine molar mass
Analyze causes for abnormal molecular masses
Explain the working of electrochemical cells
Differentiate between Galvanic Cells and Electrolytic Cells
Apply the Nernst Equation to calculate cell potential
Describe principles of batteries, fuel cells, and corrosion

practicals.title: Preparation of one lyophilic sol (starch, egg albumin, gum) and one lyophobic sol (aluminum hydroxide, ferric hydroxide, arsenous sulphide)
Dialysis of sols prepared in the previous step
Study of the role of emulsifying agents in stabilizing the emulsion of different oils
Effect of concentration and temperature on the rate of reaction between Sodium Thiosulphate and Hydrochloric acid
Study of reaction rates: Reaction of Iodide ion with Hydrogen Peroxide or Potassium Iodate and Sodium Sulphate
Enthalpy of dissolution of Copper Sulphate or Potassium Nitrate OR Enthalpy of neutralization of strong acid and base
Determination of enthalpy change during interaction between Acetone and Chloroform
Variation of cell potential in Zn/Zn || Cu/Cu with change in concentration of electrolytes (CuSO4 or ZnSO4)
Separation of pigments from extracts of leaves and flowers by paper chromatography and determination of Rf values
Preparation of double salt of Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate or Potash Alum OR Preparation of Potassium Ferric Oxalate
Preparation of Acetanilide, Di-benzal acetone, p-Nitroacetanilide, or Aniline yellow/2-Naphthol Aniline dye
Tests for functional groups: Unsaturation, alcoholic, phenolic, aldehydic, ketonic, carboxylic and amino (Primary) groups
Characteristic tests of carbohydrates, fats and proteins in pure samples and their detection in given foodstuffs
Determination of concentration/molarity of KMnO4 solution by titrating against Oxalic acid or Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate
Qualitative analysis: Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt
practicals.details: Qualitative Analysis (Section B Mandatory). Cations include Pb, Cu, Al, Fe, Mn, Ni, Zn, Co, Ca, Sr, Ba, Mg. Anions include CO3, S, SO3, NO3, NO2, Cl, Br, I, SO4, PO4.

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations. Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

visually_challenged_scheme.details: 30 minutes duration. Total of 15 questions. Student answers any 10.

mandatory_experiments_section_b: Quantitative analysis (KMnO4 titration with Oxalic acid or Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate)
Qualitative Analysis (Determination of one anion and one cation)

Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

practicals.title: Preparation of one lyophilic sol (starch, egg albumin, gum) and one lyophobic sol (aluminum hydroxide, ferric hydroxide, arsenous sulphide)
Dialysis of sols prepared in the previous step
Study of the role of emulsifying agents in stabilizing the emulsion of different oils
Effect of concentration and temperature on the rate of reaction between Sodium Thiosulphate and Hydrochloric acid
Study of reaction rates: Reaction of Iodide ion with Hydrogen Peroxide or Potassium Iodate and Sodium Sulphate
Enthalpy of dissolution of Copper Sulphate or Potassium Nitrate OR Enthalpy of neutralization of strong acid and base
Determination of enthalpy change during interaction between Acetone and Chloroform
Variation of cell potential in Zn/Zn || Cu/Cu with change in concentration of electrolytes (CuSO4 or ZnSO4)
Separation of pigments from extracts of leaves and flowers by paper chromatography and determination of Rf values
Preparation of double salt of Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate or Potash Alum OR Preparation of Potassium Ferric Oxalate
Preparation of Acetanilide, Di-benzal acetone, p-Nitroacetanilide, or Aniline yellow/2-Naphthol Aniline dye
Tests for functional groups: Unsaturation, alcoholic, phenolic, aldehydic, ketonic, carboxylic and amino (Primary) groups
Characteristic tests of carbohydrates, fats and proteins in pure samples and their detection in given foodstuffs
Determination of concentration/molarity of KMnO4 solution by titrating against Oxalic acid or Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate
Qualitative analysis: Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt
practicals.details: Qualitative Analysis (Section B Mandatory). Cations include Pb, Cu, Al, Fe, Mn, Ni, Zn, Co, Ca, Sr, Ba, Mg. Anions include CO3, S, SO3, NO3, NO2, Cl, Br, I, SO4, PO4.

competency_groups.title: Solutions
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to types, concentration expressions, solubility, vapor pressure, ideal and non-ideal solutions, colligative properties, and abnormal molecular masses.
competencies: Identify different types of solutions
Express the concentration of solutions using various units
Explain factors affecting solubility
Describe vapour pressure of liquid solutions
Distinguish between ideal and non-ideal solutions
Apply colligative properties to determine molar mass
Analyze causes for abnormal molecular masses

competency_groups.title: Electrochemistry
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to electrochemical cells, galvanic cells, Nernst equation, conductance, electrolytic cells, batteries, fuel cells, and corrosion.
competencies: Explain the working of electrochemical cells
Differentiate between Galvanic Cells and Electrolytic Cells
Apply the Nernst Equation to calculate cell potential
Analyze conductance in electrolytic solutions
Describe principles of batteries, fuel cells, and corrosion

competency_groups.title: Chemical Kinetics
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to reaction rates, factors influencing rate, integrated rate equations, temperature dependence, and collision theory.
competencies: Define the rate of a chemical reaction
Identify factors influencing the rate of reaction
Derive and apply integrated rate equations for zero and first-order reactions
Explain the temperature dependence of reaction rates using Arrhenius equation concepts
Describe collision theory of chemical reactions

competency_groups.title: d and f Block Elements
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to periodic table position, electronic configuration, general properties of transition elements, important compounds, lanthanoids, actinoids, and applications.
competencies: Locate d-block and f-block elements in the Periodic Table
Write electronic configurations for d-Block Elements
Explain general properties of Transition Elements (d-Block)
Describe important compounds of transition elements
Differentiate between Lanthanoids and Actinoids
Identify applications of d- and f-block elements
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit 4: d and f Block Elements

competency_groups.title: Coordination Compounds
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to Werner's theory, terminology, nomenclature, isomerism, bonding theories (including metal carbonyls), and applications.
competencies: Explain Werner's Theory of Coordination Compound
Define important terms pertaining to coordination compounds
Apply IUPAC rules for nomenclature of coordination compounds
Identify types of isomerism in coordination compounds
Describe bonding theories including Metal Carbonyls
Discuss importance and applications of coordination compounds

competency_groups.title: Haloalkanes and Haloarenes
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to classification, nomenclature, C-X bond nature, preparation methods, physical properties, chemical reactions, and polyhalogen compounds.
competencies: Classify haloalkanes and haloarenes based on structure
Apply IUPAC nomenclature rules for halides
Analyze the nature of C–X bond in alkyl halides

competency_groups.title: Alcohols, Phenols and Ethers
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to classification, nomenclature, functional group structures, specific properties of alcohols/phenols, commercial importance, and ethers.
competencies: Classify alcohols, phenols, and ethers

competency_groups.title: Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to nomenclature of carbonyl/carboxyl groups, preparation methods, physical properties, chemical reactions, and uses.
competencies: Apply IUPAC nomenclature for aldehydes, ketones, and carboxylic acids
Describe structures of Carbonyl and Carboxyl groups
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit 8: Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids

competency_groups.title: Amines
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to structure, classification, nomenclature, preparation methods (including Diazonium salts), physical properties, chemical reactions, and synthesis applications.
competencies: Describe the structure of Amines

competency_groups.title: Biomolecules
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to carbohydrates, proteins, enzymes, vitamins, nucleic acids, and hormones.
competencies: Classify and describe the structure of Carbohydrates
